name: Trident Palace
description: The seat of King Aurelion Veyrath IX and the political heart of Atlantis. The palace contains royal chambers, diplomatic halls, council meeting rooms, treasury vaults, and restricted archives dating back centuries.
dmSecret: Most citizens never enter beyond the outer halls.

name: Tidewarden Headquarters
description: The command center of the Tidewardens. Patrol assignments, investigations, military operations, and intelligence reports pass through this district.
dmSecret: Following graduation, this becomes one of the most important locations available to the characters.

name: Hall of the Trident King
description: The administrative district of Atlantis where officials, magistrates, record keepers, diplomats, and noble representatives conduct daily business.

name: Training Grounds
description: The primary training complex of the Tidewarden Academy where recruits master combat, tactics, athletics, survival skills, monster identification, law enforcement procedures, and military discipline.

name: Proving Pits
description: A massive public arena used for competitions, military demonstrations, sparring tournaments, obstacle challenges, tactical exercises, and graduation trials.

name: Silvered Market
description: The commercial center of Atlantis. Merchants sell weapons, armor, food, clothing, magical goods, salvaged relics, and imported supplies from across the Deep Realm.

name: Grand Armory
description: The largest military armory in Atlantis. Weapons, armor, shields, tridents, crossbows, magical equipment, and military supplies are produced and maintained here.
dmSecret: The armory is heavily guarded at all times.

name: Scholar's Deep
description: The center of learning and research within Atlantis. Libraries, laboratories, observatories, historical archives, and magical research facilities fill this district.
dmSecret: Some records are accessible to the public. Others are not.

name: Temple of the Deep Current
description: The largest religious structure in Atlantis. Shrines to many gods can be found within the temple complex. Clerics, paladins, healers, and pilgrims frequently visit seeking guidance, healing, and divine wisdom.

name: Docks of Tideshield
description: The primary transportation hub of Atlantis. The Deep Realm relies upon trained sea creatures, cargo beasts, and specialized underwater transports.
dmSecret: Most missions beyond the city begin here.

name: Barracks of Tides
description: The recruit housing district containing dormitories, training quarters, mess halls, equipment storage facilities, and recreational areas used by Tidewarden recruits.
dmSecret: The player characters awaken here at the beginning of the campaign.

name: Coral Gardens
description: A peaceful district dedicated to recreation, memorials, and public gathering spaces. Bioluminescent coral forests, statues of heroes, historical monuments, and tranquil pathways attract citizens seeking relaxation.
knownFeatures: Memorials to those lost to the sea

name: Artisan's Bellow
description: The crafting district of Atlantis. Blacksmiths, artificers, tailors, jewelers, alchemists, sculptors, and craftsmen maintain workshops throughout the district.

name: The Deep Gate
description: The main entrance to Atlantis. Massive defensive walls, military checkpoints, and customs stations protect the city from external threats.
dmSecret: For most citizens, this district symbolizes both security and the boundary between civilization and the dangers of the wider ocean.
